sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 36-year-old female, presents with a complaint of ankle pain that occurred two days ago when she slipped and twisted her left ankle on the outside, causing her to fall.
She reports swelling, pain, and difficulty walking.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation, which has provided some relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no radiation to her calf, lower leg, or knee.
She denies bleeding.
She has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes, for which she takes metformin, and high blood pressure, for which she takes Norvasc.
Her hemoglobin A1c is 6.8, which is well-controlled.
She is due for an eye exam for her diabetes checkup, which she has an appointment scheduled for in the next couple of weeks.
Review of systems: No other symptoms mentioned.
Current medications:
Metformin [dosage not specified] [frequency not specified] for diabetes
Norvasc [dosage not specified] [frequency not specified] for high blood pressure
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is normal, with no bruits.
Her lungs are clear, and her heart exam is normal, except for a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur that has been unchanged from previous exams.
Her belly exam is normal.
On her ankle exam, she has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling and redness, and pain with valgus stressing of the ankle.
She has normal sensation and pulses in her lower extremities.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an ankle sprain, with possible fracture, and is being treated with an air splint and crutches to avoid weightbearing until an x-ray can be obtained.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; suspected fracture; diabetes; high blood pressure.
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the patient's ankle to rule out a fracture.
I put her in an air splint and gave her crutches to avoid weightbearing until the x-ray results are available.
I prescribed Naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control and instructed her to keep the ankle elevated and apply ice.
I also instructed her to continue with her current medications, including metformin and Norvasc.
I ordered baseline labs, including a hemoglobin A1c, to monitor her diabetes control.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the x-ray results are available to determine the next course of treatment.
